auth.welcomeSub: Sign in to access the Arenet console.

errors.oidcGeneric: Sign-in failed (code: {code}). Try again, or use a local account.
errors.oidcNotAuthorized: This account is not allowed to sign in. Ask an administrator to add your e-mail or OIDC identifier to the access list.
errors.oidcInvalidState: The SSO request was rejected (invalid, expired or replayed state token). Try again.
errors.oidcIdpError: The identity provider rejected the sign-in request. Contact the IdP administrator.
errors.oidcIdpUnreachable: The identity provider is unreachable. Try again later, or sign in with a local account below.
errors.oidcInternal: An unexpected error occurred during SSO sign-in. Try again, or use a local account.

routes.pageSubtitle: Manage reverse proxy routes — hosts, upstreams, TLS, WAF, authentication.
routes.importCaddyfileTooltip: Phase 2 — Caddyfile import not yet wired
routes.noMatchFilter: No routes match the current filter.

dns01Banner.title: DNS-01 routes need a DNS provider.
dns01Banner.message: At least one route is configured for DNS-01 ACME, but the OVH DNS provider is missing or incomplete in
dns01Banner.tail: . Certificate renewals for these routes will fail until the provider is configured.

panel.emptyHint: Select a route on the left to edit it, or click

delete.title: Delete route
delete.confirmText: Are you sure you want to delete the route for
delete.warning: Caddy will be reloaded immediately. This action cannot be undone.

wafCRSDialog.title: Disable the OWASP CRS rules?
wafCRSDialog.message: You are about to stop loading the OWASP Core Rule Set on this route. Protection against SQLi, XSS, RCE, LFI and Protocol attacks will no longer be active. Use this only for trusted internal APIs (LAN) or legacy routes generating recurring false-positives. You can undo this action at any time by ticking the checkbox back.

form.tlsEnableHelper: Public domain required for Let's Encrypt; localhost / .local will fall back to internal CA.
form.tlsUseDedicatedCertLabel: Use a dedicated cert for this route (opt out of the wildcard)
form.tlsUseDedicatedCertHelper: Use this for routes that need a separate key (e.g. payments, staging) — the route will request its own ACME cert alongside the wildcard.
form.healthCheckHelper: Leave a field blank to use the server default (GET / 30s / 5s / passes=1 / fails=1). URI is required.
form.upstreamPrivateIPHint: Private IP + https detected — the upstream certificate is probably self-signed. Use "Advanced upstream TLS" if the connection fails.
form.tlsAdvancedHelper: Tick only if the upstream presents a self-signed certificate (homelab Proxmox, Synology DSM, ESXi, UniFi). In production, prefer adding the CA to Arenet host's trust store.
form.wafModeStart: Start with Detect to spot false positives before enforcing.
form.uploadStreamingHelper: Disables WAF inspection of the request body AND Caddy's in-RAM buffering. Enable for large uploads where the body would otherwise be staged in RAM (Docker registry, file servers, video transcoders). Request headers + URL + response stay scanned. Leave this toggle OFF for API/web routes where the body is small and should be scanned.
form.wafDisableCRSLabel: Disable OWASP CRS rules (trusted internal APIs)
form.wafDisableCRSHelper: Stops loading the OWASP Core Rule Set on this route. No more SQLi / XSS / RCE / LFI / Protocol rules. The WAF handler stays wired (dashboard counter + audit log intact) but Coraza no longer loads any rule, so none can trigger. Use only for trusted internal APIs (LAN) or legacy routes where the CRS generates recurring false-positives. The WAF mode (off/detect/block) is preserved and effective the moment the CRS is re-enabled.
form.wafExcludeRulesHelper: Disables only the listed CRS rules on this route; the rest of the Core Rule Set stays active. Use when a specific rule fires a false-positive on legitimate traffic. 6-digit IDs in [200000, 999999], excluding the [100000, 199999] Arenet-reserved range. Comma or space separators.
form.wafExcludeRulesIdentifyRules: To identify rules that fired on this route recently, see
form.wafExcludeRulesIdentifyRulesGeneric: To identify rules that fired recently, see
form.wafExcludeRulesCRSDisabledWarning: ⚠️ The CRS is disabled via the toggle above — exclusions are silent until re-enabled.
form.wafExcludeTagsHelper: Disables every CRS rule carrying the listed tag on this route. More resilient than an ID list: a CRS update that adds rules to a tag automatically inherits the exclusion. Tag = a string with no spaces or commas inside (e.g. paranoia-level/3). Autocomplete suggests 24 common tags; any valid CRS tag is accepted.
form.rateLimitToggleLabelFull: Enable rate limiting (brute-force / API abuse protection)
form.rateLimitHelper: Beyond the maximum requests per period, requests are rejected with HTTP 429. Sliding-window algorithm (no hard reset at end of period). The default key {http.request.remote.host} resolves to the raw socket peer IP (no X-Forwarded-For trust — anti-spoof). On a deployment behind a trusted proxy, switch to {http.request.header.X-Forwarded-For} or similar.
form.errorPagesTemplateHelper: The Arenet branded default applies automatically to the 401 / 403 / 404 / 429 / 500 / 502 / 503 / 504 codes if no template is attached.
form.errorPagesOverrideHelper: Overrides replace BOTH the template AND the default for the listed code. Leave blank to use the template or the default.
form.wafModeDetect: Detect — log matches, let traffic through
form.countryBlockSearchPlaceholder: Type FR, DE, Russia, United States…

certs.pageSubtitle: ACME-managed TLS certificates. Wildcard apex configuration ships under managed domains; per-route certs are auto-provisioned by certmagic when TLS is enabled.
certs.renewalHelper: All certificates are automatically renewed by certmagic (Caddy v2) ~30 days before expiry, with exponential retry on failure. No manual action required. Renewal logs are available on the {logsLink} page.
certs.certsLoadError: Could not fetch certificates (backend returned an error). The rest of this page remains usable.
certs.certsEmptyHelper: Certificates are auto-provisioned when a TLS route is created or when a managed apex is declared.
certs.staleBadgeTooltip: Last failure: {ago} · {count} attempt{plural} without successful renewal. Click to view history.
certs.policiesLead: A managed apex issues ONE wildcard certificate via DNS-01 (covers every subdomain route). The DNS provider is configured under {settingsLink}.
certs.dnsUnconfiguredHelper: Wildcard issuance is disabled — covered routes will serve self-signed certs from Caddy's internal CA until you configure the DNS provider in
certs.policiesFoot: Declaring an apex marks every existing route under *.<apex> as covered by the wildcard. The per-route ACME selector is hidden in the route editor, and the certificate is provisioned once for every subdomain.
certs.mdDeleteLead: Covered routes' ACMEChallenge will be reverted. Pick the post-revert challenge value below.
certs.mdDeleteWarning: Heads up. Each covered route will request its own HTTP-01 cert on the next reload. Many routes on one apex may hit Let's Encrypt's per-domain rate limit (50 certs / week).
certs.drillEmpty: No lifecycle event observed for this domain in the retention window (90 days).
certs.drillLead: The {count} most recent event{plural} for this domain, newest first.
certs.wizardApexHint: Bare domain (no leading *.) — the wildcard is implied. Issues a cert for {wildcard}.
certs.wizardIncludeApexLabel: Include bare apex in cert SAN

waf.pageSubtitle: The Coraza engine applies the OWASP Core Rule Set. Event counts shown below reflect blocked requests over the last 24h, per category.
waf.disabledHelper: WAF event counts are surfaced via the observability subsystem, which failed to start. Coraza is still inspecting traffic; only the dashboard projection is missing.
waf.categoriesReadOnlyNotice: Granular category control (enable/disable per category) is deferred to a future step. Counts shown below are read-only.
waf.drillEmpty: No rule triggered in this category in the last 24h.
waf.ipEnforcementHelper: The full timeline of blocked IPs (auto + manual). Manual list management is deferred (see backlog).

topology.title: Topology
topology.lede: Reverse-proxy entry points on the left, upstream services on the right. The thickness and brightness of each line reflect the real-time throughput of that flow.

sidebar.legendNote: Thickness is computed on the 60-second rolling average. The animation points in the dominant traffic direction — incoming requests towards the services.

logs.pageSubtitle: Real-time stream of WAF events, throttling, authentication failures and certificate lifecycle. Full 2xx/3xx traffic capture is deferred — see backlog.
logs.emptyNoEvents: No events in the current window.
